channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: After failed peace talks, President Trump threatens to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Less than a day after peace talks between the US and Iran failed, President Trump said that he will blockade the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: Less than a day after peace talks between the US and Iran failed, President Trump said that he will blockade the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Amid the Iran war, Ukraine makes gains against the Kremlin
item.description: With attention focused on Iran, Ukraine has made economic, battlefield and diplomatic gains against the Kremlin.
item.content:encoded: With attention focused on Iran, Ukraine has made economic, battlefield and diplomatic gains against the Kremlin.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Iran-US conflict fought amid censorships and digital fog of war
item.description: Amid the war in Iran, countries have been censoring and policing what their citizens are saying about the conflict and what information they have access to.
item.content:encoded: Amid the war in Iran, countries have been censoring and policing what their citizens are saying about the conflict and what information they have access to.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Will Orbán's defeat change Hungary's relationship to the EU?
item.description: Historic voter turnout in Hungary ousted Prime Minister Viktor Orbán from power. This shift may dramatically change Hungary's relationship with the European Union, which Orbán criticized regularly.
item.content:encoded: Historic voter turnout in Hungary ousted Prime Minister Viktor Orbán from power. This shift may dramatically change Hungary's relationship with the European Union, which Orbán criticized regularly.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat in historic Hungary election
item.description: Hungarians turned out in historic numbers to vote against Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's and his Fidesz party. NPR's Rob Schmitz discusses with reporter Esme Nicholson and political scientist Abel Bojar.
item.content:encoded: Hungarians turned out in historic numbers to vote against Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's and his Fidesz party. NPR's Rob Schmitz discusses with reporter Esme Nicholson and political scientist Abel Bojar.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: The consequences of Hungary's election, for Viktor Orban and the world
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with political scientist Abel Bojar about the results of Hungary's election, the far-reaching consequences of the vote and the future for Viktor Orban.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with political scientist Abel Bojar about the results of Hungary's election, the far-reaching consequences of the vote and the future for Viktor Orban.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: What's your favorite spy film? NPR staffers share their thoughts
item.description: NPR staffers discuss their favorite films that feature spies and espionage.
item.content:encoded: NPR staffers discuss their favorite films that feature spies and espionage.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: How families in northern Nigeria are rewriting the rules on children
item.description: Even in Africa's most populous country, attitudes about family size oare starting to shift. The change in thinking is most striking in Nigeria's Muslim and conservative north.
item.content:encoded: Even in Africa's most populous country, attitudes about family size oare starting to shift. The change in thinking is most striking in Nigeria's Muslim and conservative north.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: White House looks to Republican-led states to crack down on immigration
item.description: President Trump points to Tennessee as a model for cooperating with the immigration crackdown but some in the state don't like what it means.
item.content:encoded: President Trump points to Tennessee as a model for cooperating with the immigration crackdown but some in the state don't like what it means.
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: Pakistan hosts historic face-to-face talks between the US and Iran
item.description: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles
